task.title: Short descriptive title
task.goal: One sentence — what this task achieves when done
task.pre-conditions: - What must be true before this task can start - e.g., "zod is installed", "auth middleware exists"

scope.cannot-modify: everything else — be explicit about boundaries

task.context: Paste relevant existing code snippets, function signatures, line numbers. The agent should not need to hunt — give it what it needs here.
task.references: - docs/conventions/example.md — relevant convention - docs/adr/NNN-decision.md — why we chose this approach
task.steps: 1. Concrete step with exact file, function, and line reference 2. No "figure it out" — every step is actionable 3. Reference existing patterns the agent should follow
task.tests: - Test file: tests/path/to/test.ts - Test: descriptive test name — what it asserts - Test: another test — what it asserts
task.acceptance: - Testable condition 1 - Testable condition 2 - No regressions in existing functionality - lint ✅ types ✅ tests ✅
task.pitfalls: - Known gotchas from past issues or codebase quirks - e.g., "This framework auto-imports X — use stubGlobal, not vi.mock"
task.anti-patterns: - What NOT to do — explicit implementation warnings - e.g., "Do NOT create a wrapper — use the library directly"
task.no-mocks: - What to use real instead of mocking - e.g., "Use real zod validation — no mocking zod"
